methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $25M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
AlixLabs is a Series A-stage semiconductor company founded by Jonas Sundqvist that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in Chips & Semiconductors at the same stage, including Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M), alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD).

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $25M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage step-ups, implies a $50M valuation. Peer-median methodology anchors to the median valuation of the 4-peer sample at $1.5B, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiple (24.4x median across the 3 public tickers) cannot be applied absent disclosure of AlixLabs' revenue. Triangulating the two applicable methods yields a valuation range of $42.5M (low), $775M (median), to $1.7B (high).

The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around AlixLabs' competitive position and technical differentiation relative to a peer set with substantial valuation spread. Stage-progression suggests a more conservative floor; peer medians suggest significantly higher value if the company's progress aligns with similarly-staged competitors. Revenue disclosure would narrow this range materially.
